name: ai-commit-message
description: Generate a git commit message from your changes and fill the native Source Control input, powered by the Claude Code CLI.

enumDescriptions: Conventional Commits: type(scope): imperative subject, optional what/why body.
Free-form: a concise imperative subject with an optional body.
Auto: match the predominant language of recent commits.
aiCommitMessage.language.description: Language of the generated commit message.

aiCommitMessage.includeUnstagedFallback.description: When nothing is staged, fall back to the working-tree diff (and warn discreetly). If disabled, generation requires staged changes.

aiCommitMessage.claude.model.description: Claude model alias or full name used to generate the message. A capable model is recommended since this is a one-shot action.

aiCommitMessage.claude.executablePath.description: Path to the Claude Code CLI binary (e.g. C:\Users\you\.local\bin\claude.exe). Leave empty to auto-detect 'claude' on your PATH. Requires Claude Code to be installed and authenticated.

aiCommitMessage.fastMode.description: Prioritize speed over quality: use a fast model (claude-haiku-4-5-20251001) with extended thinking disabled.

aiCommitMessage.claude.disableThinking.markdownDescription: Disable Claude's extended thinking when generating the message. Lowers latency at a small cost to quality. Fast mode already disables thinking regardless of this setting.
aiCommitMessage.claude.disableThinking.title: Claude: Disable Thinking
